title: Chapter Thirty-Nine: Blood Lightning
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

He did not wait for an answer. He turned from the heel.

The flow connecting waist to right arm was free and powerful. He generated great power with small movement -- four ounces moving a thousand pounds, raised beyond defence into attack. A short true step, a powerful straight punch.

Gak Jeong's body staggered. Shock in his widened eyes.

The barrage began. Complex trajectories, afterimages of hands and feet tangling. Yeon-shin took more hits -- the experience gap was vast. But there was no pain. Only the joy of striking. His physical body had already surpassed the Little Divine Monk's.

From afar, an aged voice muttered continuously.

"Did he take the forms of the Shaolin Five Fists? No -- the principles are similar, but raised higher. The application of foot force has increased. How is this possible? This is like creating a new martial art..."

Master Won Jong. Hyeon Won-chang glanced at the old monk but said nothing.

The match reached its natural end. Both stepped back simultaneously, mirror images.

Yeon-shin's fists ached -- a good ache, the kind that came from striking true against a worthy body. Bruises were forming along his forearms and ribs. Gak Jeong was breathing hard, his kasaya torn at the left shoulder where Yeon-shin's final feint-and-strike had crumpled the cloth.

"If we continue, one of us will really need the Clear Righteousness Pill," Yeon-shin said.

"I learned a lesson. Let me hear the monk's dharma talk."

Gak Jeong laughed loudly -- a temple-bell sound. "Your character is truly plain. How nice it would have been to have a disciple like Sir Jeong under me. In reality, they're all so arrogant that scolding with martial arts and dharma doesn't work at all. Honestly..." His smile, which had been wide as a guardian deity's, grew strangely stiff. He continued slowly.

"I was afraid. As the fist moves gradually stopped working, it seemed they would be broken down one by one soon. Since this is my defeat too -- if Sir listens to the dharma, I too will listen to one request from Sir. Whenever."

"Good."

Yeon-shin nodded readily. Three gains today. All significant. Shaolin's fist and leg techniques experienced directly through his body -- the best kind of fertiliser for martial arts, richer than any scroll. The Clear Righteousness Pill, which could save a life once. And the right to ask the Little Divine Monk for murim matters -- a connection whose value could not yet be measured.

*Xinye White Martial Faction. The administrator. Shaolin monks. How connections continue from the strangest roots.*

Beyond the hazy light of sunset, the moon was rising white. It seemed wrong to return to the guest room tonight. Yeon-shin directed the terrified black path underlings with a jerk of his chin, and they cleaned the pavilion with frantic haste. The Xinye magistrate fled with a face the colour of old paper.

They settled in. Hyeon Won-chang distributed recovered valuables for the commoners. The administrator sat quietly in a corner, his face caught between joy and shame -- the expression of a man who had failed to protect his master's legacy but could not suppress his happiness at seeing that master's son alive and powerful.

Gak Jeong's dharma lecture filled the evening hours. Candlelight flickered against the walls as the monk spoke of the First Patriarch of Chan Buddhism -- Bodhidharma telling Huike to bring forth his anxious mind.

"When Huike said he could not find it," Gak Jeong said, his voice low and measured, "the Patriarch replied: *There. I have put it at ease for you.* The anxiety has no substance. It is a phantom that vanishes when you turn to face it directly."

Yeon-shin listened in stillness. The many fierce battles at a young age were tempering his spirit like a sword blade. But conversely, his heart -- numbed to others' lives, intertwined with his own terminal fate -- was beginning to emit killing intent too freely. He could feel it in the way he had beheaded the tavern thug without hesitation. In the cold efficiency with which he had taken the sect leader's head.

*An anxious mind has no substance.* He wanted to believe it. But his anxiety had a name. The baihui acupoint. The door that was opening whether he willed it or not.

Chan Buddhism transmits dharma through oral tradition. One dharma talk did not remove the fear. But he could learn the intent penetrating Buddhist martial arts. It was a new world.

Sky-blue light brushed past his eyes.

In a corner of the room, Master Won Jong watched him with an unreadable expression. The old monk had been studying him all evening -- not with suspicion, but with something closer to recognition. As if he saw in Yeon-shin a reflection of his own obsession.

Or perhaps his own grief.

---

Dawn. Twilight still on the horizon. Breakfast together. And the argument.

"'Destroying evil and revealing righteousness' isn't abstract dharma!" Won Jong's aged voice rang with conviction. "It was real power! Dharma power annihilates the energy accumulated by evil sects like the Blood Flame Sect! Techniques and martial arts were originally one -- the ancients' unfathomable skills divided and called two things!"

"You're creating martial arts to kill the Blood Flame Sect leader," Gak Jeong said evenly. "You who took refuge in the dharma."

Gak Jeong leaned toward Yeon-shin and whispered. "His family was destroyed by the Blood Flame Sect. He's been pouring his life into this."

*Like me.*

"How is that wrong?" Won Jong pressed. "The lost Guanyin Blue Steel Hand could crack the skulls of blood demons!"

"A lost martial art whose manual was burned? Master uncle, your words and actions--" Gak Jeong shook his head. The meal ended in awkward silence.

*Guanyin Blue Steel Hand.* Yeon-shin filed the name. *Martial arts containing dharma power. Specialised to counter blood techniques. A fundamental opposition in nature -- not just breaking technique, but opposing the energy itself.*

There existed relationships beyond what he had known. For someone whose martial level was still rising, it was a new clue. Just knowing such things existed seemed enough to create something.

He already had the forms of Shaolin fist techniques. The principles. The intent filling the dharma. To Jeong Yeon-shin, it was no different from being given everything.

---

"I wondered why you hadn't returned."

Ma Jin appeared through body technique, crossing the distance in an instant. Behind him, the rest of Radiant Demon Wing poured through the gate.

"Shaolin monks were here. No leisure for greetings -- we're being surrounded."

"Surrounded?"

"Blood Flame Sect. Information leaked. Hao Sect has betrayed us."

His tone was taut. Even for a Desolate Sect black-level, there was tension.

Yeon-shin looked around. The mountain birds had stopped singing. A soft whistle of wind crept across his cheeks. Eerie. Silent.

"We break through the main gate."

Ma Jin already held his great sword. The Radiant Demon Wing masters drew their weapons -- nine blades, a forest of steel in the dawn light. Even those who hadn't made sword-drawing sounds were tense. Yeon-shin could feel it in the quality of their breathing, in the way their energy signatures tightened like fists.

Cheong Myeong and Baek Mi-ryeo flanked him without being told. Hyeon Won-chang took position behind. The administrator and Gak Jeong moved into the centre of the formation.

They said martial world forces' encirclements covered wide territory. There was a term for it -- heaven and earth net. A net spread across heaven and earth. Nothing escaped.

"If you leave the formation, meet at the Righteous Heaven Sword Family at Mount Pingding," Ma Jin said. "Desolate Sect's Henan branch. Generous treatment if you survive."

"Was that meant as a joke?" Cheong Myeong asked, adjusting his eyepatch.

Before anyone could smile, the wall shattered.

Footsteps. Clear. Deliberate. As if walls were open gates.

A woman.

Beautiful enough to be wrong. Black hair and red eyes. An appearance never seen before. Casual, informal bearing. Her hair fluttering in the breeze was pitch black -- not ordinarily dark, but deeper than the night sky, like the robe she wore. Her lips red as if holding blood. White skin. The energy surrounding her was sinister, ominous, suffocating.

*Blood Flame Sect. Hair completely black. A master whose martial arts have reached the highest refinement.*

An apostle. The seventh.

"Seventh Apostle," Ma Jin said. His voice scraped up from deep in his gut.

"How stupid. Not even knowing about the ambush." Her voice was clear as jade. She walked forward with a bright red smile. Light steps, seductive dignity, and terrifying energy waves all at once. An atmosphere like a swamp.

"So you're Lightning Flash." Her gaze turned to Yeon-shin. Interest swirled in blood-red pupils. "Desolate Sect's future."

She smiled.

"What a pretty nape."

"Look at me, Seventh Apostle."

She glanced at Ma Jin and turned away, indifferent. Her long fingers moved.

Blood Flame Sect members revealed themselves from every direction. A hundred red-haired fighters. Twenty Blood Swordmasters. Yeon-shin's vision turned crimson.

"Here," she said. "I'll kill Radiant Demon Wing's future and present."

Her hand blurred.

She rushed like blood-coloured lightning. A bright red dagger aimed for Yeon-shin's brow.

He grabbed it while turning. His body reacted before his mind -- the physical shell responding to evil power like an immune response. The force's aftermath was monstrous, driving him back a step, but the energy in his grasp felt alien.

It dissipated.

The blood technique's true energy imbued in the dagger crumbled under his fingers. Gak Jeong's dharma rang in his ears like a temple bell.

*Forever eliminate the three poisons and always keep the six roots pure.*

Buddhist scripture. Shaolin fist technique. The principles transforming into something old and new at once. An incomplete martial art sprouting in this moment.

The Radiant Demon Wing masters who turned in surprise flinched. The sky-blue that occasionally shone in Lightning Flash's eyes had turned deep blue.

"So apostles also use Blood Flame Sect martial arts," Yeon-shin said.

The Seventh Apostle was smiling, amused. He stared at her.

"Your martial arts are easy to handle."

Blue eyes. Flashing.

---
